symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, constant abdominal pain radiating to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep constant abdominal/back pain is a hallmark of a symptomatic (expanding) AAA, though non-specific in isolation.
Back/flank pain overlaps with renal colic but lacks the classic colicky, unilateral quality.
Abdominal pain is non-specific for diverticulitis without localising left iliac fossa features.
Back pain component could be musculoskeletal in isolation but combined features argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient-reported pulsatile/throbbing sensation in the abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new patient-perceived pulsatile abdominal sensation is a strong indicator of a dilated, expanding aortic segment. Mandates AAA exclusion regardless of overall prior probability.
Pulsatile sensation is not a feature of renal colic and effectively argues against it as the primary cause.
No plausible mechanism for a pulsatile sensation in diverticulitis.
No musculoskeletal mechanism produces a pulsatile abdominal sensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain radiating from abdomen to lower back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Back radiation of abdominal pain reflects retroperitoneal stretch from an expanding aorta and is a classic feature distinguishing symptomatic AAA from many intra-abdominal causes.
Flank-to-back radiation also occurs in renal colic but typically unilateral and colicky rather than constant midline.
Back pain alone is consistent with musculoskeletal cause but the combination with pulsatility argues against it.
Presyncope in the context of new pulsatile abdominal pain suggests transient hypotension from a contained aortic leak — a marker of haemodynamic instability and impending rupture.
Simple vasovagal syncope would not typically co-occur with a new pulsatile abdominal mass sensation and constant worsening pain; the combination argues for a structural/vascular cause instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain steadily and progressively worsening since onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive, non-fluctuating worsening (rather than intermittent colicky pain) suggests an actively expanding rather than stable aneurysm, raising near-term rupture risk.
Renal colic is classically colicky/fluctuating rather than steadily progressive.
Hypertension is a well-established risk factor for aortic aneurysm formation and expansion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable pulsatile supra-umbilical abdominal mass on clinician examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable, expansile, pulsatile supra-umbilical mass is the classic examination finding for AAA, but requires hands-on palpation the telehealth AI Doctor cannot perform.
Turbulent flow through a dilated aortic segment can produce an audible bruit on auscultation, but this requires a stethoscope and clinician skill unavailable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT angiography confirming aortic dilation and any contained leak

investigation_details.expected_result: Aortic dilation greater than 3cm confirming AAA, with assessment for contained rupture/leak.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot be ordered directly via telehealth — AI Doctor should recommend ED transfer where this imaging will occur emergently.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT angiography is the definitive confirmatory investigation for AAA and any associated rupture/leak.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of new pulsatile abdominal sensation, deep constant abdominal/back pain progressively worsening, and transient presyncope constitutes a classic symptomatic AAA presentation carrying a high near-term rupture risk, regardless of confirmatory examination or imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive confirmatory investigation for AAA size and rupture status. Must occur in an emergency department, not via telehealth. AI Doctor should recommend emergency transfer where this occurs.
Rapid bedside screening tool available in ED to confirm aneurysm presence while CT is arranged; not available via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, group and hold/crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: In case of rupture requiring emergency surgical repair, blood products must be available; assesses for occult bleeding via haemoglobin drop.

Not appropriate in this acute symptomatic presentation — outpatient screening ultrasound is for asymptomatic at-risk populations, not for a patient with active pain, pulsatile sensation, and presyncope who needs emergency assessment now.
